title: CRISPRi-mediated transcriptional silencing in iPSCs for human brain development
author: Johansson, Adami, et al.

steps.title: Combine guides for analysis for reduction of off-target effects
steps.instruction: This part of the protocol involves combining or verifying the effect of the CRISPRi using two different guides. This varies depending on the read-out. RNA-sequencing is used here as readout, and the combination of guides will be described below (Figure 7).Figure 7Schematics of fbNPCs FACS and RNA-seq analysesOnce the GFP+ fbNPCs are sorted via FACS, they are prepared for RNA-sequencing. The sequencing results are then analyzed to compare transcriptional differences between the control cells and the CRISPRi cells.

steps.title: Combine guides for analysis for reduction of off-target effects
steps.instruction: Most guides will have off-targets. However, two guides are very unlikely to have the same off-targets. Thus, combining the readouts of two guides will minimize the off-target effect of each guide. Here, we combine the two CRISPRi conditions into one, and analysis is performed with control vs CRISPRi cells (in our case n=4 and 8, respectively).

description: The protocol below describes the specific steps for using CRISPRi in human induced pluripotent stem cells (hiPSCs). This protocol has also been used in fibroblasts converted to induced neurons (Pircs et al., 2022) and is likely suitable for other cell lines. We have used an all-in-one CRISPRi backbone from Addgene (Thakore et al., 2015; Addgene_71237). Molecular cloning, lentiviral production, qRT-PCR and hiPSCs culture techniques need to be established in the lab for successful application of this protocol (these techniques are not covered here). Verified qRT-PCR primers for the gene of interest and one or two housekeeping genes are also essential.
